# Dhaatrik Chowdhury — Full Site Content for LLM Indexing

> This is the extended version of llms.txt containing detailed content summaries for each page and blog post on dhaatrik.github.io. This file is intended for LLM crawlers and AI agents seeking comprehensive context about the site's content.

---

## About the Author

**Dhaatrik Chowdhury** is an Entrepreneur and AI Engineer based in India. He has approximately 9 years of experience tutoring students in physics and mathematics across various formats — from school classrooms of 30–40 students, to offline coaching centers, one-on-one mentoring, and online sessions for NRI and international students. He has guided roughly 200 students over this period.

He is also known by his nickname **"Dhaatu"**, and his students affectionately call him **"Dhaatu Bhaiyaa"** (Hindi for "Big Brother Dhaatu"). This name became the basis for his YouTube channel, **DBS Classes** (Dhaatu Bhaiyaa's Classes).

### Core Philosophy
> "Education Is Not Free BUT KNOWLEDGE IS FREE" — and must be shared with "ZERO TERMS AND CONDITIONS"

### Professional Focus Areas
- Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning engineering
- Full-stack web development (React, TypeScript, Python, TensorFlow)
- Physics simulation and orbital mechanics
- Educational content creation and pedagogy

### Social Profiles
- GitHub: https://github.com/dhaatrik
- LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/dhaatrik
- X/Twitter: https://x.com/dhaatrik
- Medium: https://medium.com/@dhaatrik

---

## Pages

### Home (https://dhaatrik.github.io/)
The landing page features a dynamic greeting based on time of day, a hero section introducing Dhaatrik as an Entrepreneur & AI Engineer, a showcase of 4 major projects (DeltaV Lab, Vellor, FuelDrop, The Infinite Intelligence), a tech stack marquee, and the 3 most recent blog posts.

### Personnel (https://dhaatrik.github.io/personnel/)
A detailed personnel file covering Dhaatrik's background, education, work experience, and technical capabilities. Styled as a classified dossier with a technical/military aesthetic.

### Pedagogy (https://dhaatrik.github.io/pedagogy/)
Dedicated to Dhaatrik's teaching philosophy and the DBS Classes YouTube channel. Contains:
- The founding story and core philosophy
- Teaching methodology (sibling-centric model, kitchen physics, outdoor experiments)
- A video archive grid of all 20 DBS Classes YouTube videos with subject filtering
- An honest reflection on the channel's cessation due to inconsistency

### Transmissions (https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/)
The blog index page listing all published articles ("transmissions") with reading times, dates, and tags.

---

## Blog Posts (Full Summaries)

### Why Dhaatrik Started: DBS Classes and the Physics of Curiosity
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/why-i-started-dbs-classes/
**Published:** 2026-06-16
**Tags:** pedagogy, youtube

Dhaatrik founded DBS Classes on October 31, 2022 after 9 years of witnessing the failures of rote memorization in the Indian education system. The channel's curriculum was built on three pillars: (1) making physics and mathematics highly intuitive, (2) teaching using first-principles thinking, and (3) bridging the gap between abstract equations and real-life observations like rotating bus wheels and curving cricket balls. The channel produced 20 video lessons covering topics like dimensional analysis, trigonometry, and basic kinematics.

### Pedagogy from First Principles: Cooking, Open Skies, and Bus Wheels
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/my-ways-of-teaching/
**Published:** 2026-06-16
**Tags:** pedagogy

A deep dive into Dhaatrik's non-traditional teaching methodology. He treated students as younger siblings rather than subordinates (the "Dhaatu Bhaiyaa" sibling-centric model), used kitchen experiments to teach thermodynamics (Leidenfrost effect, conduction/convection), moved classrooms outdoors for trigonometry and optics, and used moving bus wheels on city streets to explain angular momentum, torque, and rolling resistance. The post includes a structured "Pedagogy Pipeline" framework: Observation → Conceptualization → Intuitive Map → Mathematics. Also covers the psychology of student engagement, attention engineering, and adaptive feedback loops across different student developmental levels.

### Why Dhaatrik Stopped: An Honest Audit of Consistency and Flaws
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/why-i-stopped-youtube-classes/
**Published:** 2026-06-16
**Tags:** pedagogy, youtube

An honest post-mortem on why the DBS Classes YouTube channel stopped after 20 videos (final upload: "Point Mass is a Myth" on September 15, 2023). Root causes identified: (1) the solo production bottleneck where Dhaatrik had to fill every role from researcher to editor, (2) the illusion of "next week" where delays compounded into years, and (3) the fragility of motivation — relying on creative sparks instead of systematic production calendars. Includes a structured "Motivation vs. Systems" comparison framework. The experience taught Dhaatrik to apply systems-first thinking to his engineering work: reducing friction, lowering cognitive load, and maintaining sustainable pace.

---

## Projects

### DeltaV Lab
Real-time orbital mechanics simulation engine using RK4 integration at 50Hz. Simulates gravitational dynamics, orbital transfers, and spacecraft trajectories.

### Vellor
Offline-first note-taking application with local database synchronization. Currently in V4 development.

### FuelDrop
High-concurrency fuel delivery platform. Version 3.0.0 shipped and operational.

### The Infinite Intelligence
Multi-agent AI debate system where multiple AI agents engage in structured parallel debates.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Who is Dhaatrik Chowdhury?**
A: Dhaatrik Chowdhury is an Entrepreneur and AI Engineer from India with 9 years of teaching experience in physics and mathematics and a focus on building AI-driven software solutions.

**Q: What is DBS Classes?**
A: DBS Classes stands for "Dhaatu Bhaiyaa's Classes" — a free YouTube channel for physics and mathematics education founded on October 31, 2022 by Dhaatrik Chowdhury. It produced 20 educational videos before ceasing uploads on September 15, 2023.

**Q: What is Dhaatrik's teaching philosophy?**
A: Dhaatrik believes in first-principles teaching where students learn through observation, intuition, and real-world experimentation rather than rote memorization. His core axiom is: "Education Is Not Free BUT KNOWLEDGE IS FREE" — shared with zero terms and conditions.

**Q: Why did Dhaatrik stop uploading on YouTube?**
A: Dhaatrik stopped due to internal execution failures: solo production bottleneck, loss of focus from other projects, and relying on motivation rather than building systematic production workflows. He has publicly documented this as a personal failure and learning experience.
